finding: the WARMSTARTED in-model WMMA ffn matmul fires tensor cores AND is ALU-overhead-bound (~21 ALU/WMMA: fp16<->fp32 conversion v_cvt 128 + register marshaling v_mov 127 + addressing v_add 83, vs 16 v_wmma). MORE overhead than the amd_copy proxy (~10/wmma), not less. The earlier m.logits capture (no-WMMA, offset-clean) was the NON-WARMSTARTED FALLBACK, not the real path -- a capture error, not a real difference.
decision_tree_outcome: WMMA + ALU-overhead-bound -> address-lowering plan VALIDATED in-model; ADD a v_cvt conversion-overhead sub-lever (the in-model kernel converts fp16<->fp32 heavily).

hot_loop.note: v_mov are register broadcasts (vN,v8) clustered before global_load = d16 fp16-load register init/marshaling

epilogue_once.note: fp32->fp16 output store conversion, AMORTIZED over the K-loop -> NOT a bottleneck

loop_epilogue_split.correction: two prior framings overturned: (1) the v_cvt CONVERSION overhead is EPILOGUE (amortized), not a loop lever; (2) ADDRESS-lowering is minor in the loop (only 18 v_add). The real hot-loop bottleneck is 127 v_mov register init/marshaling tied to the strided per-element d16 fp16 loads.
loop_epilogue_split.retarget: lever = reduce the loop v_mov register overhead -- candidate: wider fp16 loads (avoid per-element d16 + their inits) via a coalesced copy (CG-W2), or a register-alloc/lowering fix. Renderer/codegen-level.

CG_W2_coalesced_copy.verdict: REFUTED (kernel-level copy-pattern change does NOT cut v_mov / vectorize)

coalesced_isa.note: v_mov UNCHANGED (~123, like strided ~120); loads still d16 -> NOT vectorized by the access-pattern change

CG_W2_coalesced_copy.measurement_confound_caught: single-eval runs read 42 / 65 / 67 TFLOPS for the SAME coalesced kernel = clock-ramp/warmth noise (AMD-decode-measurement-confounds principle). The FAIR back-to-back run (47.3 strided vs 43.7 coalesced) + the noise-free ISA are the truth. Led astray by TFLOPS momentarily; ISA + fair-compare corrected it.
CG_W2_coalesced_copy.conclusion: the 120-127 v_mov fp16-load register overhead is NOT removable by the kernel copy access pattern -> it is RENDERER-FORCED (tinygrad's fp16 d16-load lowering / per-register init), regardless of strided vs contiguous. The dependency-free prefill lever = a RENDERER change (vectorize/widen fp16 loads, eliminate the d16 register-init) -- project-level, NOT kernel-expressible (CG-W2 refuted, like CG-1/CG-R1 before it).

CG_W2b_explicit_vector_read.verdict: REFUTED -- wide fp16 loads NOT kernel/UOp-expressible (no-opts)
CG_W2b_explicit_vector_read.finding: explicit inner-8 vector reshape on the copy STILL emits 16 d16 loads + 123 v_mov (only 2 b128). Combined with CG-W2 (contiguous, also d16): NO UOp-structure change makes tinygrad emit wide fp16 loads for the copy without the UPCAST opt -- fp16 load width is OPT/RENDERER-controlled, not kernel-expressible.
CG_W2b_explicit_vector_read.conclusion: the dependency-free prefill lever (vectorize the transposing fp16 copy to kill the d16+v_mov overhead) is firmly a RENDERER/OPT-level change (CG-W3), PROJECT-LEVEL with broad blast radius + uncertain payoff (warp-uncoalescing trade). Every KERNEL-LEVEL prefill lever now refuted: POWN-1, PWLT-A2, CG-R1, CG-W2, CG-W2b.

final_kernel_level_verdict: EXHAUSTED -- pure-tinygrad prefill matmul has no kernel-level lever left on gfx1100; only a project-level renderer/opt fp16-load-vectorization change remains, or rest at PREFILL_V2 (~80% llama), or the external Tensile route (1.41x llama, dependency).

CG_W3_routeB_beam_spike.note: REFUTED the long-standing 'BEAM hangs gfx1100' wall: BEAM=2 and BEAM=8 both COMPLETED on the 1024^2 matmul AND the prefill shape (512x12288<-4096 fp16), no hang (exit 0).
CG_W3_routeB_beam_spike.verdict: BEAM does NOT hang (wall refuted) BUT does NOT find a competitive schedule -- 14-17 TFLOPS, BELOW the default heuristic (17.7) and FAR below the warmstart (48) / Tensile (66), despite OptOps.TC being in its action space. Likely test-size mis-ranking + ineffective TC application in search. So Route B (use BEAM to auto-find the copy-vectorized schedule) is CLOSED -- BEAM is not the lever.
CG_W3_routeB_beam_spike.implication: the dependency-free prefill lever falls back to Route A (hand-coded copy vectorization / two-stage copy in the scheduler+renderer), project-level. The 'BEAM-hang' premise behind many prior deferrals was FALSE, but the deferrals' conclusion (can't easily get the schedule via search) still holds -- for a different reason (BEAM is too weak, not hanging).

CG_W3_copy_unroll_SHIPPED.verdict: option 1 (extend opt tuple) WORKS: reduce-axis UNROLL creates the contiguous copy axis CG-W1.5 thought didn't exist; vectorizes copy; +3.7% dependency-free. SHIPPED. Modest (IC-served); NOT the 48->66 Tensile gap.

RouteA_A0_gatezero.finding: the existing hand-asm GEMM amd_asm_matmul.py is fp32 SCALAR FMA (14 v_dual_fmac, 0 WMMA) -> architecturally capped BELOW the WMMA path (40.7 < LLVM-warmstart 48 < Tensile 66). It is the WRONG ALGORITHM for the Tensile target.
RouteA_A0_gatezero.wmma_asm_exists: rdna4_asm_matmul.py uses WMMA (v_wmma_f32_16x16x16_f16) BUT targets RDNA4/gfx1200 -> HUNG our gfx1100 (Wait timeout; different RDNA3 vs RDNA4 WMMA operand layout). GPU recovered.
RouteA_A0_gatezero.rdna3_wmma_supported: YES - autogen rdna3/ins.py has V_WMMA_F32_16X16X16_F16; the assemble->ELF path can emit it.
RouteA_A0_gatezero.reframe: Route A's real work is NOT tuning amd_asm_matmul (fp32 FMA) -- it is WRITING A NEW RDNA3 WMMA hand-asm GEMM (port rdna4 structure to rdna3.ins + the RDNA3 WMMA register layout). This is a multi-week expert-asm build, hang-prone.
RouteA_A0_gatezero.next: A1 = build a CORRECT RDNA3 WMMA asm GEMM (mse 0), measure TFLOPS vs 48; KILL if can't beat 48 after tuning.
